name: abdi_ranaldo_effective_spread

name: garman_klass_idiosyncratic_volatility

name: short_term_reversal
rationale: Over short horizons (about one month) individual stock returns exhibit negative serial correlation, partly from liquidity provision and overreaction, so last month's losers tend to bounce and winners tend to give back gains.

name: liquidity_dollar_volume
rationale: Trading activity proxies for liquidity: more liquid, heavily traded stocks earn lower expected returns, so low-liquidity names carry a premium. With only OHLCV available, average daily dollar volume is the natural liquidity proxy for the turnover effect.
source_title: Liquidity and Stock Returns: An Alternative Test (Datar, Naik & Radcliffe, 1998)

name: abnormal_volume
rationale: Spikes in trading volume relative to a stock's own norm signal shifts in investor attention and information flow; abnormal volume predicts the cross-section of subsequent returns beyond past-return effects.
source_title: Abnormal Trading Volume and the Cross-Section of Stock Returns (EFMA, 2016)

name: low_total_vol
rationale: The low-volatility anomaly: stocks with low total return volatility deliver higher risk-adjusted returns than high-volatility stocks, contradicting the textbook risk-return tradeoff.

name: max_lottery

source_title: Maxing Out: Stocks as Lotteries and the Cross-Section of Expected Returns (Bali, Cakici & Whitelaw, 2011)

name: overnight_intraday
rationale: Anomaly returns accrue very differently overnight versus intraday: overnight (close-to-open) returns are dominated by retail/sentiment-driven demand and persist, while intraday returns behave differently, so decomposing the two predicts the cross-section.
source_title: The Cross-Section of Intraday and Overnight Returns (Bogousslavsky, 2021)
